reasoning: Billie Eilish was the 5th most-streamed artist in 2024 globally (behind Taylor Swift, The Weeknd, Bad Bunny, and Drake). While she briefly held the #1 spot for monthly listeners in mid-late 2024 following the release of 'Hit Me Hard and Soft', reaching #1 in total annual volume requires immense, sustained output and a massive back-catalog size, which Eilish lacks compared to juggernauts like Swift or Drake. Moreover, Eilish typically does not release major albums in consecutive years (HMHAS was her third album, released 3 years after Happier Than Ever). Comparatively, The Weeknd is expected to drop 'Hurry Up Tomorrow' in early 2025, Taylor Swift continues her unprecedented baseline dominance (and may release Taylor's Versions), and Bad Bunny or Drake remain perennial heavyweights. Therefore, her probability of unseating all of the historically larger-catalog artists to become the absolute top streamed artist for 2025 is very low.
